content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "field-operations",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 24,
    "domain_depth": 18,
    "data_richness": 15,
    "interaction_quality": 20,
    "ai_integration": 8,
    "mobile_first": 14,
    "performance": 22,
    "typescript_quality": 30,
    "error_handling": 16,
    "realtime": 12,
    "accessibility": 14,
    "security": 18,
    "differentiation": 10,
    "enterprise_readiness": 15
  },
  "overall_score": 17,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "field-operations-C7-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "Daily Log page missing legally defensible form sections required by acceptance criteria",
      "file": "src/pages/DailyLog.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 7 requires weather auto-population, manpower by trade table, equipment, materials, visitors, safety observations, work performed with schedule linking, draft/submitted/approved status workflow, calendar view, 'Same as yesterday' button, voice-to-text, and 90-second submission target. The current DailyLog page uses mock data arrays with no real form sections, no weather API integration, no crew entry table, no status workflow, no calendar view, and no 'Same as yesterday' functionality. This is the P0-4 roadmap item (Daily Log Speed Optimization).",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/DailyLog.tsx, completely rebuild the DailyLog component to implement the P0-4 Daily Log Speed Optimization from PRODUCTION_ROADMAP.md. BEFORE: The page renders a static list of mock daily log entries with hardcoded data, no creation form, no weather auto-population, no crew entry table, no status workflow (draft/submitted/approved), no calendar view, and no 'Same as yesterday' button. AFTER: The page should have two views toggled by tabs: (1) Calendar View showing a monthly grid where green dots = approved, yellow = submitted, orange = draft, red outline = missing days, clicking a missing day opens create form; (2) Log Entry View with sections: Weather (auto-populated from weather_cache table via useWeather hook with manual override toggle), Manpower (editable table with columns: Trade/Company, Headcount, Hours, with running totals), Work Performed (rich text with voice-to-text microphone icon), Equipment Used (table: Name, Count, Hours), toggleable 'No incidents today' and 'No visitors today' defaults (sections expand only when toggled OFF), Photo batch upload with thumbnail grid, a 'Same as yesterday' button that clones crew/equipment/visitors from the previous day's log. Status workflow: Draft -> Submitted (locks editing) -> Approved (permanently locked). Use Supabase queries to daily_logs table with project_id filter. Add sticky 'Submit Log' button at bottom. Metric cards at top: Total Logs, Days Without Gaps, Avg Crew Size, Safety Incidents This Month. Use theme tokens for all styling. No hyphens in UI text. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "field-operations-C7-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "Safety page missing OSHA-compliant incident tracking, TRIR calculation, and inspection workflows",
      "file": "src/pages/Safety.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 11 requires: Days Since Last Incident counter, TRIR calculation with industry benchmark, incident creation with severity levels (first_aid/medical_treatment/lost_time/fatality) and required photos for severity >= medical_treatment, corrective action tracking with assignees and due dates, toolbox talk digital sign-in with signature capture, certification expiration tracking with 90/60/30 day alerts, inspection templates with pass/fail/NA checklists, and OSHA 300/300A/301 form generation. The current Safety page has minimal mock data with no real incident form, no TRIR calculation, no corrective actions, no toolbox talks, no certifications tracking, and no inspection workflow.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Safety.tsx, rebuild the Safety component to implement all PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 11 requirements. BEFORE: The page shows a basic list of mock safety data with placeholder metric cards, no incident creation form, no TRIR calculation, no corrective action tracking, no toolbox talk management, no certification expiration alerts, and no inspection templates. AFTER: Add five metric cards at top: Days Since Last Incident (large counter, resets on medical_treatment+ severity), TRIR (calculated as (recordable incidents * 200000) / total hours worked, with industry average 2.8 comparison), Open Corrective Actions count, Inspections This Week count, Certifications Expiring Soon (within 30 days). Add tabbed content: Incidents tab (table with Date, Type, Severity color-coded per OSHA: first_aid=yellow, medical_treatment=orange, lost_time=red, fatality=black, Location, Status open/investigating/closed, with Create Incident modal requiring date, location, description, severity dropdown, involved persons, and photo upload required when severity >= medical_treatment), Corrective Actions tab (table linked to incidents with assignee, due date, status, overdue highlighting), Toolbox Talks tab (list with topic, date, presenter, attendee count, Create Talk modal with digital attendance sign-in), Certifications tab (personnel certs with name, cert type, expiration date, status badges: green=valid, amber=expiring within 90 days, red=expired). Query from incidents, safety_inspections, toolbox_talks, safety_certifications, corrective_actions tables. Use theme tokens. No hyphens. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "field-operations-C7-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "Field Capture page missing camera-first workflow, GPS tagging, and drawing pin linking",
      "file": "src/pages/FieldCapture.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 14 requires: camera-first UX where Capture button opens camera immediately with no form before photo, auto-metadata (GPS, timestamp, device info, weather), post-capture overlay form (Title, Notes, Tags, Link to drawing/task/punch/RFI), drawing pin placement, AI photo analysis suggestions, offline capture queue, time-lapse slider for same-location photos, and voice notes. Current page has a basic grid of mock photos with no capture workflow, no GPS integration, no drawing linking, and no offline support.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/FieldCapture.tsx, rebuild the FieldCapture component to implement PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 14 camera-first field workflow. BEFORE: The page shows a static grid of mock photo data with placeholder thumbnails, no camera integration, no GPS tagging, no post-capture form, no drawing pin linking, no offline queue indicator, and no voice notes. AFTER: Add metric cards at top: Total Captures (count), This Week (count), Pending Sync (offline queue count), Locations Covered (unique GPS clusters). Add a prominent 'Capture' floating action button (orange, 56px, bottom-right on mobile, top-right on desktop) that triggers file input with accept='image/*' and capture='environment'. After photo selection, show a post-capture overlay modal with: auto-filled timestamp and placeholder for GPS coordinates (navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition), optional Title input, optional Notes textarea with microphone icon for voice-to-text, Tags multi-select (progress, safety, quality, defect, delivery), Link To searchable dropdown (drawing/task/punch_item/rfi with entity type icons), Save button. Main content: toggle between Grid View (photo thumbnails with metadata overlay: date, time, tag pills) and Map View (placeholder map with photo pins at GPS locations). Each photo card is clickable to detail panel showing full image, all metadata, linked entities with navigation, and AI analysis placeholder badge. Add offline banner: 'X photos pending upload' when items are queued. Filter bar: by tag, date range, linked entity type. Empty state: camera icon illustration, 'No field captures yet. Start documenting site conditions with photos.' with 'Open Camera' CTA. Use theme tokens. No hyphens. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "field-operations-C7-004",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "Activity feed components missing ARIA live regions, keyboard navigation, and focus management",
      "file": "src/components/activity/ActivityCard.tsx",
      "description": "The ActivityCard component has interactive elements (entity link buttons, comment buttons, like buttons) that lack aria-labels, role attributes, and keyboard event handlers. The timestamp uses formatTimeAgo without a datetime attribute for screen readers. The type icon badges have no aria-hidden or descriptive text. The entire card clickable area has no role='article' or aria-labelledby. Real-time activity updates need aria-live='polite' region.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/activity/ActivityCard.tsx, make these specific accessibility improvements to the ActivityCard component and its sub-components. BEFORE: The EntityLabel button has aria-label={`View ${target}`} but the main card div has no role or aria attributes, the type icon span has no aria-hidden, the timestamp span has no datetime attribute or aria-label, the Like and Bookmark buttons have no aria-labels, and keyboard navigation (Enter/Space) is not handled on the card's onClick. AFTER: (1) Add role='article' and aria-labelledby={`activity-${item.id}-title`} to the outer card div. (2) Add id={`activity-${item.id}-title`} to the span containing the user name and action text. (3) Add aria-hidden='true' to the type icon span since it is decorative. (4) Add a <time> element around the timestamp with dateTime={item.timestamp.toISOString()} and aria-label={`${formatTimeAgo(item.timestamp)}`}. (5) Add aria-label='Like this activity' to the ThumbsUp button and aria-label='Bookmark this activity' to the Bookmark button. (6) Add onKeyDown handler to the main card div that triggers onClick on Enter or Space key press. (7) Add tabIndex={0} to the main card div to make it focusable. (8) Wrap the comment count display with aria-label={`${item.commentCount || 0} comments`}. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "field-operations-C7-005",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "MentionInput silently swallows project member fetch errors with no user feedback",
      "file": "src/components/activity/MentionInput.tsx",
      "description": "The useEffect that calls getProjectMembersForMention has a .catch(() => {}) that silently swallows errors. If the API call fails, the mention dropdown shows an empty list with no indication of failure. The user has no way to know mentions are broken or retry. Additionally, there is no loading state while members are being fetched, the send button has no disabled state when value is empty, and there is no character limit or validation on the input.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/activity/MentionInput.tsx, in the MentionInput component, fix error handling and add proper states. BEFORE: The useEffect fetching project members has .catch(() => {}) which silently swallows errors, there is no loading state for the member fetch, the send button is always enabled even when input is empty, and there is no error indicator when mention data fails to load. AFTER: (1) Add a `memberLoadError` boolean state, initialized to false. In the .catch block, set memberLoadError to true and console.error the actual error. (2) Add a `membersLoading` boolean state, set true before the fetch and false in both .then and .catch. (3) When showMentions is true and memberLoadError is true, show a small red text 'Could not load team members' in the dropdown area instead of an empty list, with a 'Retry' button that re-triggers the fetch. (4) When showMentions is true and membersLoading is true, show a small 'Loading team...' text with a subtle pulse animation. (5) Disable the Send button (opacity 0.5, pointer-events none) when value.trim() is empty. Add aria-disabled='true' when disabled. (6) Add aria-label='Send comment' to the send button. (7) Add role='listbox' to the mention dropdown and role='option' to each mention item. (8) Add aria-expanded={showMentions} and aria-haspopup='listbox' to the input element. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [
    {
      "title": "GPS Geofence Auto Check-in with Crew Time Tracking",
      "description": "When a superintendent's phone enters the project geofence, automatically log their arrival time. Combine with crew GPS check-in to build defensible time records without manual entry. Integrates with daily log manpower section."
    },
    {
      "title": "AI Photo Safety Scanner with PPE Compliance Scoring",
      "description": "Run every field capture photo through vision AI to detect missing hard hats, safety vests, fall protection. Generate a daily PPE compliance score per crew. Auto-create corrective actions for violations. No competitor does this natively."
    }
  ],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's field tools are desktop-adapted afterthoughts. Fieldwire leads on mobile field capture with offline and drawing pin linking, but has zero financial or safety depth. SiteSync's field-operations module currently trails both because it lacks a functional daily log form, camera-first field capture, and OSHA-compliant safety tracking. The three P0 rebuilds (DailyLog, Safety, FieldCapture) would leapfrog Fieldwire on safety depth and match Procore on documentation while delivering the mobile-first UX neither provides.",
  "summary": "The field-operations module scores 17/100 and is the weakest module in the platform. All three pages (Daily Log, Safety, Field Capture) fail their PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requirements fundamentally: Daily Log lacks weather auto-population, crew entry tables, status workflow, calendar view, and the critical 90-second submission target; Safety lacks TRIR calculation, OSHA severity classifications, corrective action tracking, toolbox talks, and certification expiration alerts; Field Capture lacks camera-first workflow, GPS tagging, drawing pin linking, and offline queue. The activity feed components have solid TypeScript patterns but need accessibility fixes (ARIA live regions, keyboard navigation) and error handling improvements (silent catch blocks in MentionInput). The five issues target: (1) rebuilding DailyLog as the P0-4 roadmap item with all acceptance criteria, (2) rebuilding Safety with OSHA-compliant incident tracking and TRIR, (3) rebuilding FieldCapture with camera-first GPS-tagged workflow, (4) adding ARIA attributes to ActivityCard, and (5) fixing silent error swallowing in MentionInput. These represent the highest-impact changes because field operations is where superintendents live 8 hours a day — if these pages don't work in the field, SiteSync fails its core value proposition."
}
```
